name: SAPÈL
description: Tamed random source. Two symmetric clusters (yellow, green) each provide three synced S&H outputs — one raw, one 2ⁿ-quantised to semitones, one n+1-quantised to octaves — plus a fluctuating output. Four noise colours (blue, white, pink, red).

controls.name: Clock Rate
controls.description: Internal clock frequency driving the S&H triggers.
controls.detail: ~0.1 Hz – 60 Hz · CV modulated via Gate/CV input

controls.name: Single / Both Switch
controls.description: Routes the other cluster's clock into this one for density mixing.
controls.detail: centre: own clock · flip: add other cluster's clock into own trigger stream

controls.name: Random Clock Mode
controls.description: Additive or subtractive blend of the random clock with the main clock.
controls.detail: up: add randoms to main · down: randomly remove triggers from main

controls.name: Gate/CV Modulation Switch
controls.description: Selects what the Gate/CV input does.
controls.detail: CV mode: modulates clock rate · Gate mode: >3 V manually triggers S&H

controls.name: Sample-and-Hold Button
controls.description: Manual trigger. Overrides clock while held. LED lit while depressed.
controls.detail: momentary · main clock out still pulses normally

controls.name: n+1 Value
controls.description: Sets the number of octaves in the n+1 quantised output pool.

controls.name: 2ⁿ Value
controls.description: Sets the exponent of 2 for the semitone-quantised output pool.

controls.name: FRV Rate / Global Rate of Change
controls.description: Speed of the fluctuating random voltage. Also biases main clock density.
controls.detail: CW: faster fluctuation + denser random clocks · CCW: slower · CV via C.3

controls.name: Probability Distribution
controls.description: Biases the voltage magnitude the S&H circuits are likely to output.
controls.detail: noon: medium voltages favoured · CCW: lower · CW: higher · does not clip the pool

controls.name: Probability Distribution Switch
controls.description: Opts each S&H into the global probability bias.
controls.detail: one per output: raw S&H, 2ⁿ, n+1, fluctuating

inputs.name: Clock Input (external)
inputs.description: External trigger/gate bypasses the internal clock for that cluster. Requires steep edges (square/pulse/sawtooth with negative ramp).

inputs.name: Gate/CV Modulation Input
inputs.description: Switch-dependent: CV modulates the clock rate, or >3 V gate manually samples the S&H cluster.

inputs.name: n+1 Value CV
inputs.description: CV modulates the number of octaves in the n+1 pool.

inputs.name: 2ⁿ Value CV
inputs.description: CV modulates the number of quantised semitone steps.

inputs.name: FRV Rate / Global Rate of Change CV
inputs.description: CV over the fluctuating rate and random-clock density.

outputs.name: S&H Output (raw)
outputs.description: Non-quantised stepped random voltage, 0 V to ~7.5 V. True analogue-noise-sampled randomness.

outputs.name: 2ⁿ Output
outputs.description: Stepped random quantised to 1/12 V semitones. Range grows with the n setting.

outputs.name: n+1 Output
outputs.description: Stepped random quantised to 1 V octaves.

outputs.name: FRV (Fluctuating Output)
outputs.description: Continuous smooth random voltage, independent of clocks.

outputs.name: Main Clock Output
outputs.description: Regular clock driving the S&H cluster. Mirrors external clock when patched.

outputs.name: Random Clock Output
outputs.description: Random clock stream. Adds or subtracts triggers from main depending on Random Clock Mode switch.

outputs.name: Blue Noise
outputs.description: Inharmonic noise with intensity rising with frequency. +3 dB/oct spectrum. Hissy, bass-deficient.

outputs.name: Pink Noise
outputs.description: White filtered at -3 dB/oct. Equal intensity per octave — balanced for the ear.

outputs.name: Red Noise
outputs.description: Brown/Brownian noise. -6 dB/oct slope. Low rumbling spectrum.

behaviors.name: Three synced values per trigger
behaviors.description: Each cluster's three stepped outputs (raw S&H, 2ⁿ semitones, n+1 octaves) update simultaneously from the same trigger. The three values are sampled from independent thermal-noise streams, so they are uncorrelated — one chord voice can have pitch, timbre, and amplitude coming from one cluster without phase-locking.

behaviors.name: True thermal-noise sampling
behaviors.description: Each S&H samples a dedicated thermal-noise source, not a pseudo-random generator. 30-minute warmup is recommended — the module needs to reach operating temperature for the noise floor to sit in the working range.

behaviors.name: Clock mix via Single/Both
behaviors.description: Routes the opposite cluster's clock into this cluster's trigger circuit. Both internal and external clocks still obey the switch. Used to densify one side or create polyrhythmic S&H patterns from two independent rates.

behaviors.name: Random clock density coupled to FRV
behaviors.trigger: FRV Rate / Global Rate of Change knob
behaviors.description: A single knob controls both the fluctuating voltage speed and the random-clock density. Turning CW raises fluctuation rate and adds more random triggers; CCW slows and thins them. CV at C.3 modulates both simultaneously — modulating tempo feel and smooth-CV speed with one signal.

behaviors.name: Additive vs subtractive random clock
behaviors.description: Up (additive): random clock outputs all main-clock pulses plus extra random triggers. Down (subtractive): randomly removes triggers from the main sequence — a gate with holes. Useful for generative percussion where you want rhythmic variation instead of density increase.

behaviors.name: Probability biases magnitude not pool
behaviors.description: Does not clip the voltage range — it biases which values are more likely within the existing pool. Unlike the n knobs (which resize the pool), probability skews frequency of occurrence. Lower-voltage values still appear when CW, just rarely.

behaviors.name: External clock bypass
behaviors.description: Patching the Clock Input disables that cluster's internal clock and the clock-rate knob. External clock must have steep rising edges — sine/triangle won't sample. Main Clock output mirrors the external clock (useful for clock cleanup).

behaviors.name: Fluctuating is always running
behaviors.description: FRV output is continuous and independent of clocks and gates. Treat it as a smooth independent LFO-rate random source that happens to share a rate knob with the random-clock density.

patchIdeas.name: Generative melody with locked octave
patchIdeas.patch: Yellow 2ⁿ set to n=2 (4 semitones per chord pool) · Yellow n+1 at n=1 (2 octaves) · clock Yellow at moderate rate · sum 2ⁿ and n+1 for pitch that stays in a 2-octave window with a 4-note set · probability knob biases toward mid-range centre of the pool.
patchIdeas.diagram:
SAPÈL:
* Yellow 2ⁿ Value: n=2
* Yellow n+1 Value: n=1
* Probability: noon
* Probability Dist Switch (2ⁿ): ON

- Clock (Out) c> SAPÈL (Ext Clock In Yellow)
- SAPÈL.Yellow (2ⁿ Out) p> Mixer (In 1)
- SAPÈL.Yellow (n+1 Out) p> Mixer (In 2)
- Mixer (Out) p> VCO (V/Oct)

patchIdeas.name: Uncorrelated pitch & timbre
patchIdeas.patch: One clock drives Yellow · Yellow 2ⁿ → VCO pitch · Yellow raw S&H → filter cutoff · both update together but sample independent noise sources · each gate produces a new pitch and a new colour, never tied.
patchIdeas.diagram:
SAPÈL:
* Yellow 2ⁿ Value: n=3

- Clock (Out) c> SAPÈL (Ext Clock In Yellow)
- SAPÈL.Yellow (2ⁿ Out) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- SAPÈL.Yellow (S&H Out) >> Filter (Cutoff)
- SAPÈL.Yellow (Main Clock Out) t> Envelope (Trig)

patchIdeas.name: Polyrhythmic pair
patchIdeas.patch: Yellow clock at slow rate · Green clock at ~3× faster · each cluster drives its own voice (pitch via 2ⁿ, trigger via main clock out) · result is two interleaved random-pitch lines with independent rhythms from one module.
patchIdeas.diagram:
SAPÈL:
* Yellow Clock Rate: slow
* Green Clock Rate: ~3× yellow
* 2ⁿ per cluster: n=3

- SAPÈL.Yellow (Main Clock Out) t> ENV 1 (Trig)
- SAPÈL.Yellow (2ⁿ Out) p> VCO 1 (V/Oct)
- SAPÈL.Green (Main Clock Out) t> ENV 2 (Trig)
- SAPÈL.Green (2ⁿ Out) p> VCO 2 (V/Oct)

patchIdeas.name: Subtractive rhythm
patchIdeas.patch: Patch external steady clock into Yellow · Random Clock Mode switch down (subtractive) · Yellow random clock out is the incoming clock with triggers randomly removed · drive a kick drum for an Autechre-style rhythm with holes · FRV Rate controls hole density.
patchIdeas.diagram:
SAPÈL:
* Yellow Random Clock Mode: subtractive
* Yellow FRV Rate: noon

- Clock (Out) c> SAPÈL (Ext Clock In Yellow)
- SAPÈL.Yellow (Random Clock Out) t> Drum (Trig)

patchIdeas.name: Smooth random modulation
patchIdeas.patch: Green FRV output at moderate rate drives filter resonance · no clock needed — FRV runs continuously · FRV Rate CV lets an envelope bend the fluctuation speed, faster during note attacks, slower on release.
patchIdeas.diagram:
SAPÈL:
* Green FRV Rate: noon

- SAPÈL.Green (FRV Out) >> Filter (Resonance CV)
- Envelope (Out) >> SAPÈL.Green (FRV Rate CV)

patchIdeas.name: Buchla 266-style voltage cascade
patchIdeas.patch: Use SAPÈL to mimic Source of Uncertainty three-output behaviour · each cluster's three outputs feed complementary parameters of one voice: n+1 → coarse pitch, 2ⁿ → fine-pitch offset, raw S&H → VCA level · probability knob biases the musical centre-of-mass.
patchIdeas.diagram:
SAPÈL:
* Yellow n+1 Value: n=2
* Yellow 2ⁿ Value: n=3

- Clock (Out) c> SAPÈL (Ext Clock In Yellow)
- SAPÈL.Yellow (n+1 Out) p> Mixer (Pitch Coarse)
- SAPÈL.Yellow (2ⁿ Out) p> Mixer (Pitch Fine)
- Mixer (Out) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- SAPÈL.Yellow (S&H Out) >> VCA (CV)

patchIdeas.name: Pink noise filtered percussion
patchIdeas.patch: Pink noise into a VCF · Main Clock Out of one cluster triggers an envelope · envelope modulates cutoff · 2ⁿ output of same cluster modulates resonance · per-hit pitched-noise percussion with correlated trigger but uncorrelated colour.
patchIdeas.diagram:
SAPÈL:

- SAPÈL (Pink Noise) -> Filter (In)
- SAPÈL.Yellow (Main Clock Out) t> Envelope (Trig)
- Envelope (Out) >> Filter (Cutoff)
- SAPÈL.Yellow (2ⁿ Out) >> Filter (Resonance)
- Filter (Out) -> VCA (In)
- Envelope (Out) >> VCA (CV)

patchIdeas.name: Ratcheting via manual S&H
patchIdeas.patch: Set Gate/CV switch to Gate · feed a burst/gate pattern into Gate/CV input · every gate high samples a new value on the Yellow cluster · main clock continues to tick underneath for parallel duties · creates ratcheted rapid-fire pitch changes synced to drum fills.
patchIdeas.diagram:
SAPÈL:
* Yellow Gate/CV Switch: Gate

- Ratchet Gen (Out) g> SAPÈL (Yellow Gate/CV In)
- SAPÈL.Yellow (2ⁿ Out) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- SAPÈL.Yellow (Main Clock Out) t> Envelope (Trig)
